skill.name: specdd-docs
skill.description: Use when Agent needs to write or update documentation from active SpecDD specs.
skill.summary: Use when Agent needs to write or update documentation from active SpecDD specs.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution and flagged the frontmatter description as weak cryptography. Review found these are false positives: the file contains documentation instructions, file path references, and skill names, not executable code. No prompt injection, network access, credential handling, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Commands
low_findings.description: The flagged lines use backticks to format file paths, skill names, and terms in markdown. They do not invoke shell commands or Ruby execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines contain markdown inline-code formatting only. No executable script, command substitution, or interpreter context is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The flagged frontmatter description names SpecDD documentation usage. It contains no hash function, cipher, credential handling, or cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain metadata text. There is no code path or cryptographic API to evaluate.

content.value_statement: Spec-driven projects need documentation that matches current contracts. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to update docs from active SpecDD specs.
actual_capabilities: Checks that a project has a root SpecDD bootstrap file before continuing.
Resolves bootstrap files in the required order for documentation work.
Builds the effective spec chain for a target path or behavior.
Identifies local spec authority before editing documentation.
Guides documentation updates without expanding the governed contract.
Reports specs used, docs changed, and underspecified behavior.
limitations: Only applies to projects that already use SpecDD.
Does not adopt SpecDD into a new project.
Does not replace human review of product or legal documentation.
Requires readable specs and implementation context for accurate output.

use_cases.title: Update Feature Documentation
use_cases.description: Refresh user or developer docs after a governed feature spec changes.

use_cases.title: Audit Documentation Claims
use_cases.description: Compare existing documentation against active specs and report unsupported promises.

use_cases.title: Prepare Release Notes
use_cases.description: Draft concise release documentation from current specs, examples, and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Find Relevant Specs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the SpecDD docs workflow for this project. Identify the active bootstrap chain and the specs that govern the documentation target.

prompt_templates.title: Update One Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the documentation for this feature using the governing SpecDD specs. Keep claims limited to the spec and implementation evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Review Documentation Drift
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this documentation against the effective spec chain. List unsupported claims, missing constraints, and suggested corrections.

prompt_templates.title: Document a Cross-Cutting Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Resolve the governing specs for these changed files, then update all authorized documentation and report remaining underspecified behavior.

output_examples.input: Update the API guide after a SpecDD change.
output_examples.output: The guide now reflects the active request and response rules. The report names the specs used and notes one missing error-case contract.

output_examples.input: Check whether this onboarding page still matches the specs.
output: Unsupported claim: automatic migration is described but not specified.
Missing constraint: manual approval is required before production use.
Recommended edit: narrow the workflow description to the governed steps.

output_examples.input: Create release documentation from the current feature specs.
output_examples.output: The release notes summarize specified behavior, preserve required limitations, and avoid promises not present in the governing specs.

best_practices: Start with the active bootstrap chain before editing any documentation.
Quote or summarize exact spec authority when documentation meaning is sensitive.
Report spec gaps instead of inventing missing product behavior.
anti_patterns: Using the skill in a project without SpecDD bootstrap files.
Expanding documentation promises beyond specs and implementation evidence.
Treating CLI summaries as the source of truth for documentation claims.

faq.question: What projects should use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it for projects that already have SpecDD bootstrap files and governed documentation workflows.

faq.question: Can it create SpecDD specs for a new project?
faq.answer: No. It is for documentation updates in existing SpecDD projects.

faq.question: Does it edit documentation automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide edits when the governing specs grant write authority for the target documentation.

faq.question: How does it prevent unsupported documentation claims?
faq.answer: It requires claims to come from active specs, implementation evidence, examples, or tests.

faq.question: Can it use CLI-assisted spec discovery?
faq.answer: Yes, when useful and available, but final documentation claims should come from specs and implementation.

faq.question: What should happen when a spec gap is found?
faq.answer: The gap should be reported instead of silently creating a new rule in documentation.
